title: Iron Snake Mode: Random Board Shapes with Level Goals
content:
## Overview

Iron Snake Mode is an optional game mode where the board is carved into a random, irregular shape instead of the usual square grid. The grid-doubling mechanic (`expandGrid`) is replaced by a level progression system: each level has a cumulative score goal, and reaching it generates a fresh board shape with a higher goal and gently increased speed. Boards also contain interior obstacle holes that the snake must navigate around — colliding with one is fatal, just like hitting the outer wall.

The mode composes with all three existing game modes (single-player, two-player, bot-vs-bot) and is off by default — when disabled, the classic game is byte-for-byte unchanged.

---

## Activation

A toggle labeled **"Iron Snake Mode"** sits at the bottom of the menu button group (below "Bot vs Bot", above the footer) in `index.html`. Its `change` listener in `setupEventListeners` sets `this.ironSnakeMode`. All three launch paths read `this.ironSnakeMode` in `startNewGame`; replay paths (Play Again, R/Enter) reuse the setting, generating a fresh shape each replay.

---

## Architecture

### Single collision chokepoint

The entire irregular-shape constraint is injected at one place: `inBounds()` in `src/main.ts`. Every collision path (player movement, `wouldCollide`, `checkCollision`, bot BFS `analyzePosition`) already funnels through `inBounds`, so rewriting its body to delegate to `onBoard(x, y)` makes the irregular shape — including interior holes — an automatic constraint everywhere with no changes to bot logic or movement loops.

- `onBoard(x, y)` — the single source of truth for playable cells: checks rectangle bounds AND (`boardMask === null` OR `boardMask[y * gridSize + x] === 1`).
- `inBounds(position)` — rewritten to `return this.onBoard(position.x, position.y)`.
- Classic mode keeps `boardMask = null`; `onBoard` short-circuits to the original rectangle check.
- Interior holes are represented as `0` cells in `boardMask`, so they are automatically fatal via the same `inBounds` path as outer walls.

### State fields (src/main.ts)

| Field | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ironSnakeMode` | `boolean` | Toggle; persists across replays |
| `boardMask` | `Uint8Array \| null` | Flat array, length `gridSize²`; `1` = playable, `0` = off-board or hole; `null` = classic rectangle |
| `boardArea` | `number` | Count of playable cells (replaces `gridSize²` as capacity metric) |
| `levelGoal` | `number` | Cumulative score threshold for the current level |

A flat `Uint8Array` is used over a `Set<string>` because `inBounds` runs in tight BFS loops — index lookup `mask[y * gridSize + x]` beats string-key hashing.

---

## Board Generator (src/iron-snake.ts)

Exports two generators:

- **`generateIronSnakeBoard(gridSize, rng?)`** — used at level 1 / `startNewGame`
- **`growIronSnakeBoard(gridSize, level, snakeCells, rng?)`** — used at level transitions; accepts a protected-cell set

Both return `{ mask: Uint8Array; area: number } | null`, falling back to `null` so callers can always fall back to a full rectangle.

### Algorithm: overlapping rooms

Several rectangles (minimum side 3) are placed with overlap to guarantee connectivity, then validated:

1. Place a first room near center.
2. For each additional room (3–6 total): pick an existing on-board cell, choose random width/height (min 3), position the rectangle to cover that cell, fill with `1`.
3. **Connectivity:** BFS over 4-connected on-board cells; prune to the largest connected component.
4. **Area check:** reject if fill ratio is outside `[targetLo, targetHi]`.
5. **Min-width check:** morphological open with a 3×3 element (erode then dilate). If the open removes any cell, that cell sat in a <3-wide sliver → reject.
6. Retry up to 30 times. Return `null` on exhaustion.

Validated across 8,000 trials with 100% connectivity and zero corridor failures.

**Why overlapping rooms over cellular automata or random walks:** Rectangles of minimum side ≥ 3 make the no-narrow-corridor property nearly free by construction. Overlap guarantees connectivity. Area is easy to tune.

### Interior holes (`carveHoles`)

After the solid shape passes validation, `carveHoles()` punches small interior voids into the mask. Holes are validated before being committed using the same connectivity check and morphological open that guard the base shape — no new safety logic was written.

Key design decisions:

- **Anchoring on known playable cells:** Each placement attempt is anchored on an actual on-board cell, and the entire hole footprint must be surrounded by playable neighbors ("interior only" constraint). This makes hole placement reliable on small early boards where deep-interior positions are a minority, avoiding wasting the attempt budget on off-board positions (the playable blob covers ~57% of the bounding grid).
- **Level-scaled count and size:** Hole count and maximum footprint scale with the current Iron Snake level. Early boards get one or two small single-cell gaps; later boards get several larger cavities (up to roughly 4×4 cells by level 8–10). This provides a natural difficulty ramp without a separate tuning pass.
- **Protected cells at level transitions:** `growIronSnakeBoard` builds a protected-cell set covering every snake cell plus its orthogonal neighbors before carving, so a hole can never open under a snake or immediately in front of a head when play resumes.
- **Reusing existing validators:** Any hole that survives is automatically guaranteed to be a genuine interior gap with adequate clearance. The minimum-width check rejects holes placed within one cell of another wall, making thin corridors and edge bites impossible without extra code.

### Naming history

The module and all identifiers went through two user-driven renames: `arena`/`randomArena` → (intermediate) → `ironSnake`/`ironSnakeMode`. A `grep -i arena` check confirmed zero mixed vocabulary remains.

---

## Level / Goal System

Replaces `checkGridExpansion()` when Iron Snake Mode is active. The dispatcher at the two `checkGridExpansion()` call sites (single-player ~line 1015, multiplayer ~line 1084) routes to `checkLevelAdvance()` instead.

### Goal formula

```
goalForLevel(L) = 5 · L · (L + 1) / 2
```

Cumulative thresholds: **5, 15, 30, 50, 75, …**  
Per-level deltas: **5, 10, 15, 20, 25, …** — each level strictly harder than the previous by a fixed additional amount.

**Why triangular curve:** Deltas grow linearly, which feels fair and predictable. A flat increment is too easy at high levels; an exponential is too punishing.

**Why cumulative thresholds (not per-level resets):** Goals are thresholds on the running total score. The leaderboard score is always the true final score. Each goal is crossed exactly once as the snake keeps eating. No confusing score reset mid-game. The combined `score + score2` threshold handles multiplayer with no extra UI.

### Level advance (`advanceLevel`)

On `score >= levelGoal` (single) or `score + score2 >= levelGoal` (multiplayer):

1. `level++`
2. Regenerate board mask via `growIronSnakeBoard` (fall back to `null` on generator failure)
3. Update `boardArea`
4. Reposition snake(s) to length 1 via `findStartCell`
5. `spawnFood()`
6. `levelGoal = goalForLevel(level)`
7. Ramp speed gently at **10% per level** (`baseSpeed * 0.9^(level-1)`, floored) — not halving
8. Restart the game interval
9. `updateUI()`

`gridSize` does **not** change in Iron Snake Mode, so the canvas never balloons and `isoCache` stays valid.

---

## Snake Placement (findStartCell)

`findStartCell(preferredRegion, occupied) → { pos, dir }`: among on-board unoccupied cells nearest `preferredRegion`, picks one with a direction giving ≥ `FORWARD_CLEARANCE` (~3) clear cells ahead, preventing immediate wall collisions.

- **Single-player** (`startNewGame`, `advanceLevel`): `findStartCell(centroid, ∅)`
- **Multiplayer** (`startNewGame`): P1 → left-third centroid; P2 → right-third centroid with P1 cells occupied
- Mutual reachability is guaranteed — the mask is always a single connected component
- Falls back to classic rectangle placement if `findStartCell` returns null

---

## Food Spawning

`spawnFood` uses `onBoard` in both the empty-cell filter (high-fill branch) and the rejection-sample `while` condition. The branch selector compares against `boardArea` (not `gridSize²`). An empty-list guard prevents infinite loops on very small boards.

---

## HUD

When Iron Snake Mode is active, `updateUI` replaces the grid-size display (`#grid-size`) with a live countdown:

```
To goal: N pts  (or "1 pt")
```

This decrements as the player eats and resets on level advance. The countdown naturally handles the multiplayer combined-score case with no extra UI.

---

## Rendering

Render routines guard on `onBoard` so void cells (including interior holes) show the dark background:

- `drawGroundPlane`: `if (!onBoard(gx, gy)) continue`
- `drawGridLines`: when mask active, stroke per-cell edges (deduplicating shared edges) instead of full spans
- `drawGroundBorder`: when mask active, stroke every edge where an on-board cell meets an off-board cell or the rectangle boundary, tracing the true outline — this also correctly outlines the edges of interior holes

`isoCache` covers the full `(gridSize+1)²` lattice and remains valid for any subset; `drawBlock`/`getBlockHeight` are unchanged.

---

## Bots

Bots are **shape-unaware** — `BotState` (`bot-types.ts`) is unchanged. Bot moves are engine-validated through the mask-aware `inBounds`, so a stale heuristic kills the bot rather than corrupting state. Edge-hugging bots (explorer, edge-runner, spiral, sweeper, ambusher, zigzag) degrade gracefully on irregular shapes and around interior holes. This is acceptable per requirements.

---

## Leaderboard

Iron Snake Mode records into the same `single|pvp|bvb` leaderboard boards (`leaderboard.ts` untouched). Tagging entries `ironSnake: true` is a possible follow-up, not built.

---

## Files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/iron-snake.ts` | Board mask generator (overlapping rooms, flood fill, morphological open, hole carving) |
| `src/main.ts` | `onBoard`, `inBounds`, `findStartCell`, `checkLevelAdvance`, `advanceLevel`, render guards, food spawning, HUD, dispatcher |
| `index.html` | Iron Snake Mode toggle, HUD element |
| `src/style.css` | Toggle and HUD styling |
